context_text: The second time was when she had fought against the Great Oz himself, and driven him out of the Land of the West.

context_text: The Winkies were sorry to have them go, and they had grown so fond of the Tin Woodman that they begged him to stay and rule over them and the Land of the West.

context_text: "This was all we ever had to do until the Golden Cap fell into the hands of the Wicked Witch of the West, who made us enslave the Winkies, and afterward drive Oz himself out of the Land of the West.

context_text: He had once met the Winged Monkeys in the Land of the West, and he did not wish to meet them again.
